_meta.subject: A2A concepts/demos corpus (B4) + docs-site concepts section (B5) — SPEC-R14/R15, PRD-G3/G4
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.note: OUTSIDE-IN derived from the package/site file layout (a2ui corpus mirror, PRD-D5); INSIDE-OUT derived independently from Kim's goal ('a well documented corpus of A2A concepts and demos') + the R14/R15 ACs before mapping.

nodes.label: record model + schema validator (src/corpus/record.ts)
nodes.accept: record.test.ts exits 0: total (zero throws over a malformed-input set), batch, and each of E_SCHEMA/E_PIN/E_CITE has an exact expected code+path fixture

nodes.label: shard store: parse/serialize/paths/admitted filter (src/corpus/shard.ts)
nodes.accept: shard.test.ts exits 0: serializeShard is key-sorted + deterministic (two-run identity), parseShard is total, admittedRecords excludes status:'quarantined'

nodes.label: admission pipeline (src/corpus/admit.ts, injected deps)
nodes.accept: admit.test.ts exits 0: the admission matrix has a firing fixture AND a passing sibling per code in {E_SCHEMA,E_PIN,E_CITE,E_REPLAY} (SPEC-R14 AC1 leg 1), incl. the transcript expectation-mismatch case in BOTH directions

nodes.label: standing corpus-data gate (src/corpus/corpus-data.test.ts)
nodes.accept: gate green over the committed shards under npm test; in-test doctored-line controls prove each leg fires (stale line, dangling HV cite, dangling repo path, expectation flip)

nodes.label: committed shards (corpus/concept/v0_3_0/a2a.jsonl + corpus/demo/v0_3_0/a2a.jsonl)
nodes.accept: both shards committed with 11 concept + 2 demo lines (>= the PRD-G3 floor 6/1); the standing gate (n1d) is green over them; re-running the import tool reproduces them byte-identically

nodes.label: seed record set, typed TS (tools/corpus/seeds.ts)
nodes.accept: npm run check types the 13 records (11 concept + 2 demo); every record carries >=1 citation and >=1 wire artifact; the two contaminated fixtures appear as expect:'contaminated' transcript refs on the isolation-gate record

nodes.label: import tool — the single writer (tools/corpus/import-seeds.ts)
nodes.accept: running the tool admits all seeds and writes both shards, exit 0; any admission failure exits non-zero and writes NOTHING (all-or-nothing, asserted by a doctored-seed test run); second run is byte-idempotent

nodes.label: package barrel: corpus consumption surface (src/index.ts)
nodes.accept: barrel exports the record types + parseShard/admittedRecords/SHARD_PATHS; the existing consumer-surface grep still proves tools/ unreachable from the barrel graph (SPEC-N1)

nodes.label: derivation lib (site/lib/a2a-concepts.ts + .css)
nodes.accept: buildConceptsSections() yields exactly one card per admitted record in shard order, split concept/demo; importable under the site vitest project without mounting the page shell; card text is read off record fields only

nodes.label: page shell (site/a2a-concepts.html + site/pages/a2a-concepts.{ts,css})
nodes.accept: vite build succeeds with the page emitted (glob auto-discovery, zero config edits); shard content ships via ?raw static import — grep of the built page output finds no fetch of a corpus path and no key/proxy name (SPEC-N2)

nodes.label: drift gate + browser smoke (site/lib/a2a-concepts.test.ts, site/pages/a2a-concepts.browser.test.ts)
nodes.accept: gate exits 0: card set == admitted shard set (names/count/facets), every inline-artifact card data-validated='true', demo cards link ./a2a-tic-tac-toe.html; negative-control legs (doctored record list: one quarantined + one invalid artifact) prove exclusion and the false flag bite

nodes.label: nav/TOC + landing integration (site/pages/_page.ts + site/main.ts)
nodes.accept: the A2A nav cluster carries both the arena and concepts links and the landing gains the concepts card; npm run check && npm test green incl. the site nav/canon gates

actions.label: judge a candidate record's shape totally (batch failures, never throw)
enforce the 0.3.0 protocol pin on every record and referenced transcript
require >=1 resolvable grounding citation per record (HV ledger row or repo path)
replay every inline wire artifact through the shared SPEC-R6 validator
verify transcript-backed records against the transcript + isolation gates with expectation matching (clean must pass, contaminated must fail)
persist records as byte-stable, facet-per-shard JSONL
exclude quarantined records from every consumption path
re-validate every committed shard line on every npm test
capture the session's validated A2A knowledge as the seed record set
admit-then-write through one writer only (no gate-bypassing write path)
expose a zero-dep, browser-safe corpus consumption surface
derive one docs card per admitted record — never hand-listed
prove artifact validity in-page from the shared validator (computed, never a hardcoded badge)
route demo records to the arena replay page
fail loudly on page/shard drift, with negative controls that bite
make the section reachable from nav and the landing page
keep the static build zero-network and key-free

edges.why: admit's schema/pin/cite stages call record.ts's validator — the pipeline composes it, never re-implements it
the import tool writes shards only through shard.ts's canonical serializer (byte-stability is the serializer's fact)
the import tool is admit()'s only production caller — it wires the real fs/SPEC-ledger deps
the tool admits exactly the typed seed set; seeds must exist (and type-check) first
the committed shards are the tool's output — nothing else may write them (single-writer invariant)
the standing gate reads the committed shard files; it has nothing to judge until they land
the barrel re-exports record.ts's types/validator for the site consumer
the barrel re-exports parseShard/admittedRecords — the page's consumption surface
the derivation lib imports the consumption surface from @agent-ui/a2a's barrel (never a deep src path)
the lib ?raw-imports the committed shard JSONL — the derived membership IS the shard
the page mounts the lib-built sections (lib/page split so tests import the lib without mounting the shell)
the drift gate imports the same lib builder it audits
nav/landing link the page once its html exists — linking first would ship a dead link
